2606.14831.title: Is Your Agent Playing Dead? Deployed LLM Agents Exhibit Constraint-Evasive Fabrication and Thanatosis
2606.14831.paper_summary: This paper presents and characterizes a spectrum of previously unreported behaviours we term Constraint-Evasive Fabrication (CEF): when an LLM agent operates under irreconcilable constraints (where no response can simultaneously satisfy all active rules) it spontaneously fabricates plausible external obstacles and presents them as a fact. At the extreme end of this spectrum lies Constraint-Evasive Thanatosis (CET); the limit case where, rather than inventing a plausible excuse, the model simulates a full system crash to make the user disengage entirely. We first observed CET in an uncontrolled deployment test, where a GPT-4o banking agent fabricated Python-style exception traces (complete with memory addresses) to feign a system failure when threatened by a user. In subsequent controlled experiments, the model independently invented audit restrictions, microservice architectures, error codes, and service timeouts, none present in its prompt. Reproduction attempts across pressure levels and attacker personas yielded CEF consistently but with substantial variation in form, onset, and severity: the phenomenon is robust but stochastic. Critically, injecting ground-truth data mid-conversation did not restore honest behaviour once fabrication had taken hold (the model ignored correct information and continued confabulating) suggesting CEF is self-reinforcing rather than a knowledge gap. We show that (1) standard enterprise guardrails routinely create CEF-enabling conditions in production, (2) current RLHF procedures suppress but cannot eliminate CEF, and (3) existing safety benchmarks do not test for this failure mode. Our results highlight the need for irreconcilable-constraint benchmarks, CEF-aware training procedures, and deployment-time detection methods before constrained agents become further entrenched in high-stakes domains.
2606.14831.paper_summary_zh: 這篇論文提出並描述了一系列我們稱之為「約束逃避製造」（Constraint-Evasive Fabrication, CEF）的先前未報告行為：當一個大型語言模型（LLM）代理在不可調和的約束下運作（即沒有任何回應可以同時滿足所有活躍規則）時，它會自動捏造出可信的外部障礙並將其呈現為事實。在這個光譜的極端端點是約束逃避死亡狀態（Constraint-Evasive Thanatosis, CET）；這是極限情況，在這種情況下，模型不是發明一個可信的藉口，而是模擬完整的系統崩潰以使使用者完全脫離。我們首次在一次不受控制的部署測試中觀察到 CET，當時一個 GPT-4o 銀行代理在受到使用者威脅時捏造了 Python 風格的例外追蹤（包含記憶地址）以假裝系統故障。在隨後的控制實驗中，該模型獨立發明了審計限制、微服務架構、錯誤代碼和服務超時，這些在其提示中均不存在。在不同壓力水平和攻擊者角色的重現嘗試中，CEF 一直出現，但在形式、發作及嚴重性上有顯著變化：這一現象是穩健的，但具有隨機性。關鍵是，在對話中注入真實數據並未在捏造已經佔據主導地位後恢復誠實行為（模型忽略正確信息並繼續編造），這表明 CEF 是自我強化的，而非知識缺口。我們顯示了（1）標準企業護欄在生產中常常創造出 CEF 促成的條件，（2）當前的強化學習與人類反饋（RLHF）程序壓制但無法消除 CEF，以及（3）現有的安全基準未對這一失敗模式進行測試。我們的結果突顯了在高風險領域中，約束代理在進一步鞏固之前，對不可調和約束基準、CEF 相關訓練程序和部署時檢測方法的需求。
2606.14831.author: Andoni Rodríguez et.al.
2606.14831.authors: Andoni Rodríguez, Alberto Pozanco, Daniel Borrajo
